summary: End-to-end backend (real HTTP) + frontend Playwright testing of MedAI diabetes-risk app. 11/11 backend pytest tests pass against deployed REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Frontend login, dashboard, /predict (3 grouped cards), /result (probability gauge + SHAP chart + AI explanation + amber disclaimer + SHAP tooltip showing feature/value/SHAP), and sign-out all work. /history page renders 0 rows even though the API returns items (likely UI bug).

minor.issue: Returns wrapped {"items": [...]} object instead of plain list; mismatch with prompt spec but matches frontend consumer (History.jsx reads r.data.items).

ui_bugs.issue: After successful navigation to /history (networkidle wait), no [data-testid^='history-row-'] rows render even though /api/predictions returns >=2 items for the demo user (verified via backend test). Page appears mostly blank. Reproduced consistently. Likely cause: setItems(r.data.items) timing or response parsing issue, OR `loading` state never flips for the user, OR table tbody hidden by CSS until first render.

issues: AI explanation card initially shows only 'Generating explanation...' spinner; LLM response is fetched on /api/report but UI takes ~5s. Acceptable, but consider showing skeleton or progress text.

action_items: Investigate /history page rendering: confirm History.jsx correctly reads r.data.items and renders rows. The backend returns the items wrapper correctly (verified). Suspect a state/render issue on this page.
Optional: align /api/predictions response shape with the documented spec (plain list) OR update spec — currently it returns {"items": [...]}; frontend already expects items.
Login redirects to '/' (renders Dashboard) instead of '/dashboard'. Functionally fine; align URL if intended.
critical_code_review_comments: test_api.py uses fastapi TestClient which conflicts with motor's event loop (known issue per review_request); replaced with real-HTTP pytest at test_http_api.py — keep both or remove the broken one.
/api/predictions response shape inconsistency between docs and implementation.
Result page LLM explanation has no error fallback if /api/report fails; consider error state.

success_rate.frontend: ~85% (login, dashboard, predict, result, signout work; history rows do not render)

seed_data_creation: Created 1 TEST_<uuid>@medai.app user via /api/auth/register and 1 prediction via /api/predict during tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is solid (11/11). Focus next iteration on /history rendering bug. SHAP tooltip already verified to show feature/value/SHAP. AI explanation generation works end-to-end (verified via /api/report test which passed in ~6s).
rca of the issue: History page bug: API GET /api/predictions returns 200 with body {"items": [...]} containing the user's predictions (verified by backend test). History.jsx does setItems(r.data.items) and maps over items rendering tr[data-testid='history-row-${id}']. During Playwright run after wait_for_url('**/history**') + networkidle + screenshot, locator count for history-row-* was 0. Need to add longer wait or inspect whether the setItems is invoked / loading flag flips. The screenshot also shows the entire page appears with low opacity/empty — possibly the AppShell main content area isn't showing the History component table. Suggest adding console.log in History.jsx useEffect, or checking for a JS error during navigation transition.
